response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong full-stack engineering fundamentals and 14+ years of professional experience meeting educational requirements, the resume shows no evidence of expertise in the core competencies required for this role: SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture analysis, deep neural network optimization, or AI accelerator hardware-software co-design. The career trajectory is rooted in application and backend software development, with limited hardware architecture exposure, creating a substantial gap between current expertise and principal-level hardware performance engineering responsibilities.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering experience with demonstrated systems-level thinking across microservices and distributed architecture
Proven performance optimization expertise through low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby and various infrastructure optimization projects
Some hardware exposure through National Instruments measurement software and robotic arm control, providing foundational hardware affinity
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, design, or simulation experience—a core responsibility of the role
Absence of GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD analysis, or AI accelerator optimization background—critical preferred qualifications
No demonstrated expertise in deep neural network training/inference optimization or AI workload characterization

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware design background
Strong general software engineering and systems thinking foundation
